intitule: Present Perfect Simple: Forms and Uses (Resource Task)

enonce.texte: Read each sentence carefully. Based on the rules of the Present Perfect Simple (experience, recent action, duration with for/since, or result), identify the correct form or use.

questions.texte: Which sentence correctly expresses a past action with a present result?

sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. This is Past Simple (completed action at a specific past time).

sousQuestions.texte: She has lost her keys. She cannot open the door.
sousQuestions.reponse: Correct. The past action (losing keys) has a result in the present (cannot open the door).

sousQuestions.texte: She is losing her keys now.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. This is Present Continuous (action in progress now).

questions.texte: Identify the sentence that uses 'for' and 'since' correctly.

sousQuestions.texte: I have lived in Douala since five years.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. 'Since' is used with a specific point in time (e.g., since 2019, since Monday).

sousQuestions.texte: I have lived in Douala for 2020.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. 'For' is used with a duration (e.g., for two hours, for five years).

sousQuestions.texte: I have lived in Douala for five years. / I have lived here since 2019.

questions.texte: Choose the correct form to complete the sentence: 'He ______ (never / eat) fufu and njama njama before.'

sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. Past simple would be used for a specific past time (e.g., 'He never ate it yesterday').
Correct. 'Never' + Present Perfect expresses an experience (or lack of it) in an unspecified period.
Incorrect. Present Continuous is for plans or actions in progress.

questions.texte: Which sentence correctly uses 'just' with the Present Perfect?

sousQuestions.texte: The teacher has just left the classroom.
sousQuestions.reponse: Correct. 'Just' indicates a very recent past action.

sousQuestions.texte: The teacher just left the classroom yesterday.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect. 'Just' cannot be used with a specific past time like 'yesterday'.
Incorrect. This is Present Continuous (action happening right now).

intitule: Job Interview: Experience and Recent Actions (Competence Task)

enonce.texte: Context: You are a young Cameroonian graduate applying for a job at a microfinance bank in Yaoundé. The interviewer asks you questions about your life. Complete the dialogue with the correct verb tense (Present Perfect or Past Simple). Then answer the reflection question.

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue: Interviewer: '______ you ever ______ (participate) in a community development project?' You: 'Yes, I have. I ______ (help) organize a clean-up campaign in my neighborhood last year.'

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the tense used for 'ever' and justify why 'last year' requires a different tense.
sousQuestions.reponse: 'Have participated' is Present Perfect because 'ever' asks about any time in life (experience). 'Helped' is Past Simple because 'last year' is a specific, finished past time.

questions.texte: Interviewer: 'How long ______ you ______ (know) how to use accounting software?' You: 'I ______ (know) how to use it since my final year at university.'

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the use of 'since' in your answer.
sousQuestions.reponse: 'Since' is used with the Present Perfect to indicate the starting point of an action that continues to the present (my final year until now).

questions.texte: Interviewer: '______ you ______ (receive) our email about the job requirements?' You: 'Yes, I have. I ______ (read) it two days ago.'

sousQuestions.texte: Why is Present Perfect used in the first part, but Past Simple in the second part?
sousQuestions.reponse: Present Perfect ('Have you received') asks if the action is done (relevance to present). Past Simple ('read') is used because 'two days ago' specifies exactly when the action happened.

questions.texte: Based on this dialogue, write one sentence summarizing your skills using 'for' or 'since'.
questions.reponse: Example: 'I have used Microsoft Excel for three years.' or 'I have been interested in finance since 2020.'

intitule: Present Perfect vs. Past Simple: News Report (Competence Task)

enonce.texte: Context: You are a journalist for 'Cameroon Tribune'. You are writing a short news article about a recent event. Read the draft and correct the mistakes related to the use of Present Perfect and Past Simple.

questions.texte: Find and correct the error: 'The Minister of Secondary Education has visited our school last week.'
questions.reponse: Incorrect: 'has visited' + 'last week' (specific past time). Correct: 'The Minister of Secondary Education visited our school last week.'

questions.texte: Find and correct the error: 'I never saw a better performance than the one at the Bilingual High School this morning.' (The morning is not over).
questions.reponse: Incorrect: 'never saw' (Past Simple) for an incomplete time period. Correct: 'I have never seen a better performance than the one at the Bilingual High School this morning.'

questions.texte: Complete the headline: 'The Government ______ (just / announce) a new scholarship program for girls in STEM.'

sousQuestions.texte: Why is 'just' commonly used with the Present Perfect in news headlines?
sousQuestions.reponse: Because 'just' emphasizes that the action happened a few moments ago, making the news fresh and relevant to the reader.

intitule: Life Experiences: Write About a Friend (Resource & Competence Mix)

enonce.texte: Task: Your pen pal in Nigeria asks you about your best friend in Cameroon. Using the prompts below, form correct Present Perfect sentences. Then classify them as 'Experience', 'Recent Action', or 'Duration'.

questions.texte: Prompts: 1. (He / visit / Kribi) → He has visited Kribi. 2. (She / just / finish / her exam) → ______. 3. (They / be / friends / 2015) → ______.
questions.reponse: 2. She has just finished her exam. 3. They have been friends since 2015.

sousQuestions.texte: Classify sentence 1 (He has visited Kribi) as Experience, Recent Action, or Duration.
Classify sentence 2 (She has just finished her exam).
Classify sentence 3 (They have been friends since 2015).
